title: Masquerade Reveal — Multi-Pass Post-Processing Suite
description: A masked Michelle stands in a dimly lit masquerade hall. The user orchestrates a dramatic reveal sequence by layering and tuning post-processing effects — bloom, vignette, chromatic aberration, film grain, color grading, and a selective depth-of-field — that progressively unmask and illuminate the scene through 8 validated interaction steps.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load Michelle.glb as the central character posed at origin and venice_mask.glb positioned and scaled to sit on her face like a masquerade mask. Use a dark ambient environment with a single warm spotlight from above-right and a dim blue fill light from the left to evoke a masquerade ballroom mood. Camera at (0, 1.5, 2.5) looking at head height.

Implement an EffectComposer pipeline with these passes managed via a dat.GUI panel: (1) UnrealBloomPass — user sets threshold (0-1), strength (0-3), radius (0-1); validate strength must be ≥0.5 before proceeding. (2) A custom ShaderPass for vignette with uniforms for darkness (0-2) and offset (0-2); validate darkness ≥0.8. (3) A custom ShaderPass for chromatic aberration with a uniform for pixel offset (0-10); validate offset is between 2 and 8. (4) A custom ShaderPass for film grain with intensity (0-0.5) and speed uniforms animated by time; validate intensity ≥0.1. (5) A custom ShaderPass for color grading with hue-shift (-PI to PI) and saturation (0-2) uniforms; validate saturation is between 0.5 and 1.5. (6) BokehPass for depth-of-field with focus, aperture, maxblur controls; validate focus is within 0.001-0.01 range.

Add a 'Reveal' button that, when all 6 validations pass, triggers an animation: the venice_mask slowly floats upward and rotates away from Michelle's face over 2 seconds while bloom strength auto-animates to 2.0 and vignette fades to 0, creating a dramatic unmasking. Display a green checkmark overlay on completion. Each GUI folder should show a red/green indicator for its validation state. Animate Michelle with a subtle idle breathing motion using sin-based Y-scale oscillation. The scene background should be a dark gradient via a custom background shader.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomStrength: number,         // current bloom strength value (0-3)
  bloomThreshold: number,         // bloom luminance threshold (0-1)
  bloomRadius: number,         // bloom blur radius (0-1)
  vignetteDarkness: number,         // vignette darkness intensity (0-2)
  vignetteOffset: number,         // vignette radial offset (0-2)
  chromaticAberrationOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration pixel offset (0-10)
  filmGrainIntensity: number,         // film grain noise intensity (0-0.5)
  colorGradingSaturation: number,         // color grading saturation multiplier (0-2)
  colorGradingHueShift: number,         // color grading hue rotation in radians (-PI to PI)
  bokehFocus: number,         // depth-of-field focus distance
  bokehAperture: number,         // depth-of-field aperture size
  effectsValidated: boolean,         // true when all effect parameters pass validation
}
